_description: Non-table-backed controlled vocabularies used across the extraction schema

ipcc_action_type._source: IPCC AR6 Chapter 14 adaptation action typology

values.name: Structural/Physical
values.description: Engineered and built environment, technological, ecosystem-based adaptation

values.name: Social
values.description: Educational, informational, behavioral

values.name: Institutional
values.description: Economic, laws/regulations, government policies and programs

values.name: Ecosystem-based
values.description: Nature-based solutions, green infrastructure, ecological restoration

implementing_actor_type._source: Derived from case library and CDP data analysis
implementing_actor_type._note: Use multiple actor types in arrays instead of 'multi_stakeholder' to preserve specific actor information

values.name: Anecdotal
values.description: Qualitative reports without systematic measurement

values.name: Measured
values.description: Quantitative data collected but not rigorously controlled

values.name: Rigorously Evaluated
values.description: Controlled studies, peer-reviewed evaluations

values.name: Effectiveness Indicator
values.description: A measure of how well the solution reduces the target hazard or vulnerability

values.name: Co-benefit
values.description: A secondary positive effect beyond the primary adaptation goal

values.name: Failure Mode
values.description: A documented way the solution failed or underperformed

values.name: Maladaptation
values.description: The solution worked as intended but transferred or increased risk elsewhere

values.name: Other
values.description: Outcome type not covered by the above values

co_benefit_category._source: Extended in v0.2 from CDP action_cobenefits with finance-oriented categories per Salkin framing

values.name: Case Library
values.description: Curated research from the existing 200+ case library

values.name: CDP
values.description: City self-reported data from CDP disclosure

values.name: Academic
values.description: Peer-reviewed journal articles, conference papers

values.name: Government
values.description: Government reports, plans, policy documents

values.name: News
values.description: News articles, press releases

values.name: NGO
values.description: Reports from NGOs, international organizations

values.name: Measured
values.description: Quantitative data from direct measurement

values.name: Modeled
values.description: Output from simulation or modeling

values.name: Anecdotal
values.description: Qualitative or informal observation

values.name: Self-reported
values.description: CDP and similar self-disclosure. Default for CDP data.

values.name: Expert Assessment
values.description: Professional judgment without systematic data

mechanism_vocabulary._description: Authoritative controlled vocabulary for mechanism dimension (v0.3 — upgraded from guidance-only seed). Extractors must use these values; use 'other' + free-text for outliers.
mechanism_vocabulary._note: v0.3: added restore, relocate, buffer, insulate, regulate + other. Status changed from guidance-only to authoritative. Sprint 4: added sense_and_detect, forecast_and_model, automate_and_control, inform_and_alert. 'monitor' is retained for general observation but now complemented by more specific digital-process types. See decisions-log.md §15.

values.name: Absorb
values.description: Buffer, attenuate, or absorb the impact (e.g., green infrastructure absorbing stormwater)

values.name: Redirect
values.description: Divert, channel, or redistribute the hazard (e.g., flood channels, drainage rerouting)

values.name: Harden
values.description: Strengthen, fortify, or protect against impact (e.g., building hardening, seawalls)

values.name: Monitor
values.description: Observe, measure, or track conditions over time without automated response (e.g., manual sensor readings, periodic surveys, environmental monitoring programs)

values.name: Govern
values.description: Regulate, plan, coordinate, or incentivize (e.g., building codes, zoning, carbon pricing)

values.name: Shift Risk
values.description: Transfer, insure, or redistribute risk (e.g., parametric insurance, risk pooling)

values.name: Adapt Behavior
values.description: Change practices, educate, or build awareness (e.g., demand response, heat action behavior campaigns)

values.name: Restore/Regenerate
values.description: Rehabilitate, restore, or regenerate natural or built systems (e.g., ecosystem restoration, urban forest regeneration)

values.name: Restore
values.description: Bring a degraded ecosystem or system back to a prior functional state (e.g., wetland restoration, riparian zone recovery). Use when restoration is the primary mechanism, distinct from broader regeneration.

values.name: Relocate
values.description: Move people, assets, or infrastructure away from the hazard zone (e.g., managed retreat, infrastructure relocation out of flood plains)

values.name: Buffer
values.description: Create protective zones or transition spaces that reduce hazard intensity before it reaches assets (e.g., coastal buffer strips, urban green belts, windbreaks). Distinct from 'absorb' — buffering acts at a distance from the asset.

values.name: Insulate
values.description: Create thermal or physical barriers that prevent hazard transmission into a system (e.g., thermal insulation against heat, acoustic barriers, flood-proof walls on individual structures). Distinct from 'harden' — insulation prevents transmission rather than strengthening resistance.

values.name: Regulate
values.description: Apply rules, standards, permits, or enforcement mechanisms to control hazard-relevant behaviors or land use (e.g., floodplain zoning enforcement, emission limits, water use restrictions). More specific than 'govern' — focused on rule-based compliance mechanisms.

values.name: Sense and Detect
values.description: Actively sense environmental or system conditions and detect discrete events or threshold crossings using digital sensors, IoT devices, or remote sensing (e.g., flood sensors triggering alerts, wildfire detection cameras, structural health monitoring)

values.name: Forecast and Model
values.description: Use computational models, AI/ML, or simulation to produce forward-looking predictions or scenario analyses (e.g., hydrological models forecasting flood depth, digital twins simulating heat distribution, ML-based drought prediction)

values.name: Automate and Control
values.description: Execute closed-loop machine actuation or automated system control in response to sensed conditions without requiring human decision in the loop (e.g., automated pump activation during storm events, DERMS load balancing, smart irrigation responding to soil moisture sensors)

values.name: Inform and Alert
values.description: Disseminate information, warnings, or targeted communications to populations, responders, or decision-makers to trigger human action (e.g., heat-wave SMS alerts, early-warning broadcasts, real-time dashboard for emergency managers). Distinct from automate_and_control — the response is human.

values.name: Other
values.description: Mechanism not covered by the above values. Use with mechanism_other_description.

financing_model._usage: FUNDED_BY.financing_model (v0.2 binding — CapitalProject → FinancingSource edge property)
financing_model._note: v0.2: bound to FUNDED_BY edge on CapitalProject → FinancingSource. Previously unbound.

values.name: Grants
values.description: Grant funding from government, foundations, or international organizations

values.name: Municipal Budget
values.description: City's own budget and resources

values.name: National Government Funding
values.description: National-level public finance

values.name: Regional Funds and Programmes
values.description: Regional development banks, EU funds, etc.

values.name: Public-Private Partnership (PPP)
values.description: Structured partnership between public and private sectors

values.name: Commercial Bank Loans
values.description: Loans from commercial banking sector

values.name: Development Bank Financing
values.description: World Bank, regional development banks, etc.

values.name: Climate Funds
values.description: Green Climate Fund, Adaptation Fund, etc.

values.name: Green Bonds
values.description: Municipal or corporate green bonds

values.name: Blended Finance
values.description: Mix of public, private, and philanthropic capital

values.name: Private Sector Investment
values.description: Direct private investment

values.name: International ODA
values.description: Official development assistance

accreditation_modality._source: Adaptation Fund (AF) and Green Climate Fund (GCF) access modalities for adaptation finance. Captures the institutional arrangement through which a financing source delivers capital to a project, distinct from the financing model (the structural form of the funds).
accreditation_modality._note: v0.1.1: added per peer review recommendation that AF/GCF terminology (NIEs, direct access, pooled facilities) be modeled. Belongs on FinancingSource — distinct from the operational mechanism_vocabulary, which describes how a Solution physically achieves adaptation effect.

values.name: National Implementing Entity (NIE)
values.description: AF/GCF-accredited entity within the recipient country with direct access to climate finance

values.name: Regional Implementing Entity (RIE)
values.description: AF/GCF-accredited regional entity (e.g., regional development bank) with direct access on behalf of multiple countries

values.name: Multilateral Implementing Entity (MIE)
values.description: International accredited entity (UNDP, World Bank, regional MDBs) channeling AF/GCF funds — the international-access default

values.name: Direct Access
values.description: Recipient-country institution applies for and receives funds directly without an MIE intermediary

values.name: Enhanced Direct Access (EDA)
values.description: GCF modality devolving project-level decision-making to in-country accredited entities; deeper subsidiarity than baseline direct access

values.name: Pooled Facility
values.description: Multiple sources or instruments aggregated into a single funding facility serving a portfolio of projects (e.g., national climate funds, blended-finance vehicles)

values.name: Readiness / Preparatory Support
values.description: Capacity-building and project-preparation grants (GCF Readiness, AF Readiness Grants) — funds the preparatory work rather than the adaptation project itself

values.name: Not Applicable
values.description: Source is outside the AF/GCF accreditation framework (e.g., commercial loan, municipal budget, private investment)

financing_status._note: v0.2: bound to CapitalProject and Action. Previously unbound.

values.name: Fully Funded
values.description: Complete funding secured

values.name: Partially Funded
values.description: Some funding secured, seeking additional

values.name: Seeking Full Funding
values.description: No funding secured, seeking complete funding

values.name: Seeking Partial Funding
values.description: No funding secured, seeking partial funding

values.name: Feasibility Stage
values.description: Feasibility studies and finance-ready project preparation in progress

values.name: Not Applicable
values.description: Financing not required or not relevant

equity_focus._source: EDF equity framework (Shi 2024 rubric) — adapted for solution-level classification

values.name: None
values.description: No equity framing documented in the solution

values.name: Co-Benefit
values.description: Equity benefits are secondary — not the design target, but documented as a co-benefit for vulnerable populations

values.name: Primary Target
values.description: Solution is explicitly designed to serve, protect, or empower vulnerable populations

justice_dimension._source: Shi (2024) equity rubric — four justice dimensions for climate adaptation outcomes
justice_dimension._note: Applied to Outcome nodes to characterize what type of equity impact is documented. Multiple dimensions can apply to a single outcome. See decisions-log.md §16.

values.name: Distributive
values.description: Who bears the costs and who receives the benefits — distributional fairness of outcomes and burdens

values.name: Procedural
values.description: Who participates in and shapes decisions — fairness of decision-making processes

values.name: Recognitional
values.description: Whose knowledge, history, and lived experience are acknowledged and meaningfully incorporated

values.name: Epistemic
values.description: How knowledge is produced, what counts as valid evidence, and whose expertise is legitimized
Policy mandates or requires the action
Policy or mechanism enables or supports the action
Policy issued by an actor
Actor implements the action
Policy or condition blocks or constrains the action
Action constrained by regulation or resource limitation

instrument_type._source: CDP project finance vocabulary, extended in v0.2 with US municipal CIP terms
instrument_type._note: v0.2: extended with US municipal CIP language (general_obligation_bond, revenue_bond, tax_increment_financing, special_assessment, federal_cost_share, state_cost_share, municipal_capital_appropriation, ratepayer_funded). All previous values retained.

values.name: Green Bond
values.description: Municipal or corporate green bond

values.name: General Obligation Bond
values.description: Municipal bond backed by the full faith and credit (and taxing power) of the issuing government. Per Salkin: 'no one is using this' for resilience — opportunity area.

values.name: Revenue Bond
values.description: Municipal bond backed by revenues from a specific source (water rates, tolls, etc.). Includes enterprise/ratepayer-supported financing.

values.name: Tax Increment Financing (TIF)
values.description: Bond or special-district financing repaid from the future incremental tax revenue generated by improvements in a defined area

values.name: Special Assessment
values.description: Levy on property owners within a defined benefit district to pay for improvements that directly benefit them (e.g., flood-control assessment districts)

values.name: Sustainability-Linked Loan
values.description: Loan with sustainability performance targets

values.name: Climate Bond
values.description: Bond earmarked for climate projects

values.name: Resilience Bond
values.description: Bond tied to adaptation/resilience outcomes

values.name: Blended Finance
values.description: Mix of public, private, philanthropic capital

values.name: Revolving Fund
values.description: Loan fund with repayments recycled into new loans (e.g., state Clean Water SRF)

values.name: Grant
values.description: Non-repayable funding

values.name: Federal Cost Share
values.description: Federal funding covering a portion of project cost (e.g., FEMA BRIC, USACE cost-share, FHWA federal-aid)

values.name: State Cost Share
values.description: State funding covering a portion of project cost

values.name: Municipal Capital Appropriation
values.description: Direct appropriation from the city's own capital budget — 'pay-as-you-go' rather than bond-financed

values.name: Ratepayer Funded
values.description: Funded directly through utility rates (Salkin's 'enterprise/revenue-supported pass-through to ratepayers'). Distinct from revenue_bond — ratepayer_funded is current-revenue funding, not debt issuance secured by future ratepayer revenue.

values.name: Direct Allocation
values.description: Direct public budget allocation without a structured instrument

values.name: Outcome Bond
values.description: Bond whose repayment or interest rate is contingent on achieving measurable adaptation outcomes (e.g., flood damage avoidance, heat mortality reduction). Linked via CONTINGENT_ON relationship to Outcome nodes.

values.name: Other
values.description: Instrument type not covered by the above values. Use with instrument_type_other_description.

asset_class._source: Adapted from US municipal capital-budget asset categorization (BLS public infrastructure schema, GFOA capital-asset taxonomy).
asset_class._note: v0.2: introduced with CapitalProject. Local annotation from the budget document; when the project's solution class is resolvable via REALIZES Action → IMPLEMENTS Solution → OPERATES_ON UrbanSystem, the multi-hop path is authoritative.

values.name: Other
values.description: Asset class not covered above. Use with asset_class_other_description.

construction_phase._note: v0.2: introduced with CapitalProject. Distinct from Action.status (which is plan-commitment status: committed/in_planning/under_implementation/completed/cancelled). A capital project has its own lifecycle independent of plan commitment.

values.name: Proposed
values.description: Listed in the CIP but pre-design

values.name: Design
values.description: Engineering and design underway

values.name: Permitting
values.description: Awaiting regulatory permits or approvals

values.name: Under Construction
values.description: Construction in progress

values.name: Commissioned
values.description: Project complete and in service

values.name: Decommissioned
values.description: Asset retired from service

municipal_program._source: Municipal capital-program categories common across US cities
municipal_program._note: v0.2: introduced. Captures cross-program ripple effects — when a resilience project produces outcomes that benefit other municipal programs (transit, parks, public safety, pensions, etc.). Per Salkin: 'show how investments in these solutions connect to other capital programs.'

value_recurrence._note: v0.2: introduced. Distinguishes one-time avoided losses from recurring annual benefits from lifetime-integrated value, so that monetized_value_usd can be interpreted correctly.

values.name: One-time
values.description: Lump-sum value (e.g., avoided damages from a single hazard event)

values.name: Annual
values.description: Recurring per-year value (e.g., reduced stormwater treatment cost)

values.name: Lifetime
values.description: Value integrated over the asset's useful life
